home.btrfsTips: Only folders in Btrfs volumes support the snapshot feature.
home.clearTip: Refer to {clearTip2} for the cause of invalid snapshot. It is recommended to clear them if not needed.

home.notAllowOperate: Operation failed. The folder contains a subvolume (possibly a legacy system user folder). Please delete the subvolume first
home.savePlanTip: The current snapshot retention policy will clear the {0} existing snapshot files in the folder. Do you want to save?
home.savePolicyDesc: Specify the number of snapshots kept or retention time. When the limit is exceeded, system will automatically delete old or expired snapshots to free up storage space. After deletion, it may take some time for space reclamation.
home.snapshotLimitTip: If the number of snapshots reaches the limit, system will be unable to take new snapshots. It is recommended to set a snapshot retention policy.
home.snapshotPlanDesc: Set a scheduled snapshot plan. The system will automatically take snapshots at the configured frequency while running.

snapshot.cloneShareDesc: New shared folder will be created in the same volume.
snapshot.cloneTips: Snapshot cloning is unavailable for user folder
snapshot.deleteTip: Snapshots cannot be restored after deletion.
snapshot.lockTips: Locked snapshots will not be automatically deleted
snapshot.makeSnapshotSuccess: Operated successfully. You can view details in the snapshot list
snapshot.name: Name
snapshot.resetHomeWarnDesc: The current user has enabled "Hide my personal files from other users." To proceed with restoration, contact the user to disable this option in "Files" > "Personal Folder Management."
snapshot.restoreDesc1: 1. Restore the folder to the snapshot state. A snapshot will be taken automatically before restoring
snapshot.restoreDesc2: 2. Make sure there are no ongoing tasks (upload, move, copy, decompress, etc.) in the folder during restore to avoid data loss
snapshot.restoreDesc3: It is recommended to use "Clone" for safer data recovery
